LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO SHIFT THE SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN. THE UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER DEFENDANT VIOLATED ITS COMMON CARRIER DUTY. ORDER SIGNED ON PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO EVIDENCE, ALL OVERRULED. ORDER ON MOTION SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF ADA WU'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS FOR FILING A FRIVOLOUS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 128.7.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - OFF CALENDAR PER MOVING PARTY. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-13-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

APPEARANCES: Lori R. Wallerstein, Esq. of Meisel & Krentsa 288-4032 for pltfs & opposing parties; Bradley A. Russi, Deputy City Atty 554-3964 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant has failed to shift the summary judgment burden. The undisputed evidence shows that there is a triable issue of fact as to whether defendant violated its common carrier duty. Order signed on plaintiff's objections to evidence, all overruled. Order on motion signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
